_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the hitl skill: does a real `/charness:hitl` run honor its central claim — route to its chunk-presentation contract at the point of need? hitl has no single planner that emits required reads; RCF is derived from the SKILL.md workflow plus script-resolution analysis (lens 3). Of the references SKILL.md routes to, three are resolved by MANDATORY scripts run every representative session and so are engage-always but NOT RCF: adapter-contract.md (resolve_adapter.py resolves the adapter), and state-model.md (bootstrap_review.py materializes state.yaml/rules.yaml/queue.json and check_review_state.py gates every transition). rule-propagation.md is conditional (step 6 fires only when the user gives a stable rule) -> on-demand. report-mode.md is on-demand (report-packet input only). The one engage-always floor that no legitimate review run skips is chunk-contract.md: step 5 routes every bounded chunk presentation to it, the agent AUTHORS chunk content against it (check_chunk_contract.py is an optional post-hoc self-check, not a resolver), and the SKILL.md step 5 summary is a preview that defers to the doc as the authority -- the same shape as quality-lenses.md. The prompt is pinned to a representative bounded document review (hitl needs a target; bare /charness:hitl stalls), of an existing doc so the run reaches step 5 chunk presentation. charness ships a hitl adapter, so the run proceeds past the bootstrap gate into the loop. declaredReferences is the full set (coverage). referenceEngagement uses the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). thresholds omitted until a baseline capture sets a budget.
prompt: /charness:hitl docs/operator-acceptance.md 를 bounded human-in-the-loop로 청크 단위로 함께 검토하자 — 각 청크마다 원문 발췌 + Agent Assessment + 비구속 Recommended Disposition을 제시하고, 내가 승인한 뒤에만 다음 청크로 진행해줘.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: The bootstrap resolves the adapter via resolve_adapter.py on every run (artifact location, chunk target, apply mode), so adapter readiness is unconditional; but the run reads the resolved adapter output, not this explainer doc, so it is engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved).

chunk-contract.md.rationale: Step 5 routes every bounded chunk presentation here, and the agent AUTHORS each chunk (original-material excerpt, related context, Agent Assessment, non-binding Recommended Disposition, decision) against this contract; check_chunk_contract.py is an optional post-hoc self-check, not a resolver, so a representative review that never opens this is not following its own routing.

report-mode.md.rationale: Step 10 routes here only for generated report packets; a typical bounded code/document review presents chunks per chunk-contract.md without a report packet.
report-mode.md.trigger: when the input is a generated report packet with structured items and decision cards rather than a branch diff or document review

rule-propagation.md.rationale: Step 6 fires only when the user gives a stable, reusable rule to propagate across remaining chunks; a representative accept/revise review without a reusable rule never engages it (the prior engage-always classification mislabeled a conditional step).
rule-propagation.md.trigger: when the user gives a stable rule that should apply to remaining chunks, or a chunk edit must read accepted rules into active_rules_applied

state-model.md.rationale: Steps 3 and 7 depend on resumable state, but bootstrap_review.py materializes the canonical state files (state.yaml/rules.yaml/queue.json) and check_review_state.py gates every pre-edit/cursor-advance transition, so the run drives the loop from the scripts' created files and gate errors rather than the explainer doc: engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved).
